steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: In $3x^{3}+9$, the coefficients are $3$ and $9$, whose greatest common factor is $3$. The variable $x$ appears only in the first term, so it is not part of the common factor.

steps.title: Factor and test each choice
steps.content:
Factor: $3x^{3}+9=3(x^{3}+3)$. Now expand each choice and compare to $3x^{3}+9$:
  • A)$9 \times (x^3 + 9)$ $9(x^{3}+9)=9x^{3}+81$. The leading coefficient is $9$, not $3$, so this is not equivalent.
  • B)$9 \times (x^3 + 3)$ $9(x^{3}+3)=9x^{3}+27$. The leading coefficient is $9$, not $3$, so this is not equivalent.
  • C)$3 \times (x^3 + 9)$ $3(x^{3}+9)=3x^{3}+27$. The constant term is $27$, not $9$, so this is not equivalent.
  • D)$3 \times (x^3 + 3)$ $3(x^{3}+3)=3x^{3}+9$. This matches the original expression exactly. That matches Choice D — $3 \times (x^3 + 3)$.
